objective: Build a V0 data acquisition and labeling system for dog seizure sensor IMU data.

in_scope: ESP8266 and MPU-6050 raw IMU acquisition
Sliding-window dataset preparation for future categorical models

implementation_rules: Use device-relative milliseconds as the source of truth for sample and event timing.
Join labels to samples by session_id and device_ms overlap.
Store human labels as time ranges that can be converted into sliding-window examples.
Do not treat unlabeled time as normal activity unless it has been explicitly labeled.
Write each IMU batch and its samples in one atomic database transaction.
Return 409 Conflict for duplicate device_id/session_id/sequence batches.
Do not commit private config, local databases, or real sensor data.
Do not implement seizure detection or medical alerting in V0.

tasks.title: Create repo structure
acceptance_criteria: Required folders and placeholder files exist.
data/ is present without tracking real data files.

tasks.title: Add root gitignore
acceptance_criteria: Runtime files, databases, private config, PlatformIO build output, and notebook checkpoints are ignored.
data/.gitkeep remains trackable.

tasks.title: Add README
acceptance_criteria: README explains objective, hardware, architecture, local setup, firmware setup, server startup, and V0 limitations.
A developer can run the server from the README instructions.

tasks.title: Create FastAPI app
acceptance_criteria: uvicorn app.main:app --reload starts successfully from server/.
GET /health returns 200 with {"status":"ok"}.

tasks.title: Add Python dependencies
acceptance_criteria: pip install -r requirements.txt works.
Requirements include FastAPI, Uvicorn, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, python-dotenv, pandas, matplotlib, pytest, and httpx.

tasks.title: Add health endpoint test
acceptance_criteria: pytest passes.
Health endpoint test verifies {"status":"ok"}.

tasks.title: Implement SQLite connection
acceptance_criteria: Database path is configurable by environment variable.
Default DB path from server/ is ../data/seizure_sensor_v0.sqlite.
Database parent directories are created automatically.
Starting the app creates the SQLite database and tables.

tasks.title: Implement SQLAlchemy models
acceptance_criteria: Models exist for Device, Session, Batch, IMUSample, and Event.
Event model stores session_id, start_device_ms, and end_device_ms.
Models match the plan schema and create without import cycles.

tasks.title: Create Pydantic schemas
acceptance_criteria: Schemas exist for IMUSampleIn, IMUBatchIn, IMUBatchAck, EventIn, and EventOut.
Invalid payloads are rejected by FastAPI validation.
Valid sample payloads parse successfully.

tasks.title: Implement IMU upload route
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/imu/batch validates request bodies.
Valid payload writes one batch row and N sample rows.
device_ms is stored as device_ms_start plus sample dt_ms.
raw_payload_json preserves the original batch payload.
Batch metadata and samples are committed atomically.
Duplicate device_id/session_id/sequence returns 409 without duplicate samples.

tasks.title: Add IMU upload tests
acceptance_criteria: Valid batch returns 200.
Empty samples are rejected.
Missing required fields are rejected.
Duplicate sequence returns 409.
Stored sample count equals payload sample count.

tasks.title: Implement event routes
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/events creates manual labels.
GET /api/v1/events lists labels.
GET /api/v1/events/{event_id} returns one label.
Event payloads include session_id, start_device_ms, and end_device_ms.
Event type is restricted to the V0 allowed set.
start_device_ms must be less than end_device_ms.
Severity is null or an integer from 1 through 5.

tasks.title: Add event tests
acceptance_criteria: Valid event creation succeeds.
Invalid event type is rejected.
End before start is rejected.
Event listing returns created events.

tasks.title: Implement sample export endpoint
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/export/samples returns CSV.
CSV includes accel_mag and gyro_mag.
Filtering by session_id works.
Optional start/end filters operate on device_ms or clearly documented timing inputs.

tasks.title: Add labeled export script
acceptance_criteria: Script exports a session from the command line.
Optional label join uses session_id and device_ms overlap.
Output writes to data/exports/ and opens correctly in pandas.

tasks.title: Create session plotting script
acceptance_criteria: Script accepts --db-path and --session-id.
Script computes accel_mag and gyro_mag.
Script saves session plots to data/plots/.

tasks.title: Create event plotting script
acceptance_criteria: Script accepts --event-id and configurable padding.
Script loads event windows using session_id and device_ms overlap.
Script saves event-centered plots to data/plots/.

tasks.title: Create PlatformIO project
acceptance_criteria: PlatformIO project builds for a generic ESP8266 or ESP-12E/NodeMCU-style board.
Config example documents Wi-Fi SSID, password, server URL, and device ID.

tasks.title: Implement I2C scanner mode
acceptance_criteria: Serial monitor prints detected I2C addresses.
MPU-6050 appears at 0x68.

tasks.title: Read MPU-6050 data
acceptance_criteria: Serial output shows changing accel and gyro values at approximately 50 Hz.
Still sensor acceleration magnitude is near 1 g.
Motion causes visible changes.

tasks.title: Implement Wi-Fi connection
acceptance_criteria: Device joins Wi-Fi reliably.
Firmware prints local IP.
Reconnect logic does not block permanently.

tasks.title: Implement sample batching
acceptance_criteria: Firmware samples at 50 Hz.
Firmware prepares 50-sample batches once per second.
Each batch includes device_id, firmware_version, session_id, sequence, sample_hz, device_ms_start, and battery_mv.
Sequence number increments monotonically.

tasks.title: Implement HTTP POST upload
acceptance_criteria: Firmware posts JSON to {SERVER_URL}/api/v1/imu/batch.
Server receives real sensor batches.
Server response is printed over serial.
Failed upload does not crash firmware and future uploads continue.

tasks.title: Run long firmware test
acceptance_criteria: Device runs for 30 minutes without firmware crash.
Server receives roughly 90000 samples at 50 Hz.
Missing sequence numbers are identifiable.
No obvious memory leak appears during runtime.

tasks.title: Create local web workbench
acceptance_criteria: Workbench runs locally against the FastAPI server.
User can list sessions and choose one recording.
User can inspect accel axes, gyro axes, accel magnitude, and gyro magnitude on a shared timeline.
Timeline supports zooming and panning.
A 15-minute session remains usable in the browser.

tasks.title: Add workbench API endpoints
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/sessions returns session metadata.
GET /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/samples returns range-filtered or downsampled timeline data.
PATCH /api/v1/events/{event_id} edits existing labels.
DELETE /api/v1/events/{event_id} deletes mistaken labels.
Workbench does not read SQLite directly.

tasks.title: Implement timeline event labeling
acceptance_criteria: User can create a label by selecting start and end points on the chart.
User can assign event type, severity, source, and notes.
User can choose from allowed categories including seizure, scratching, scooting, walking, running, resting, and unknown.
Existing labels render as timeline overlays.
Saved labels use session_id plus device-relative start and end milliseconds.

tasks.title: Add window generation script
acceptance_criteria: Script reads one or more labeled sessions from SQLite.
Script supports configurable --window-ms and --stride-ms.
Script can generate 2 second / 100 sample windows with 0.5 second stride for 50 Hz data.
Script can generate 5 second / 250 sample context windows with 1 second stride for 50 Hz data.
Output includes session_id, device_id, window_start_device_ms, window_end_device_ms, label, and overlap_ratio.

tasks.title: Implement overlap label assignment
acceptance_criteria: Windows overlapping one human label by at least 80 percent receive that event type.
Unlabeled windows remain unlabeled and are not treated as resting or normal.
Windows overlapping multiple incompatible labels are marked mixed or excluded by a documented option.
Train/test guidance preserves session/day/dog-level splits instead of random row splits.
All windows from the same labeled event remain in the same train/test partition.

tasks.title: Add baseline feature extraction for feature models
acceptance_criteria: Feature output includes accel and gyro magnitude statistics.
Feature output includes per-axis summary statistics.
Feature output includes jerk, RMS, percentile, signal-energy, and peak-count style features where practical.
Generated feature CSV opens in pandas.
Feature generation is reproducible from the raw SQLite database.
Feature matrix is suitable for RandomForest first and LightGBM/XGBoost comparison later.

tasks.title: Document model and labeling design
acceptance_criteria: Document explains human label semantics and multi-scale sliding-window settings.
Document explains overlap label assignment rules.
Document explains why unlabeled time is not normal by default.
Document recommends RandomForest first with LightGBM/XGBoost comparison.
Document defines general activity and seizure-like candidate review heads.
Document describes future prediction review workflow.

tasks.title: Create synthetic activity checklist
acceptance_criteria: Checklist covers still table, rotations, gentle shake, aggressive shake, walk, resting, scratching, scooting if safely observable, and shaking off.
Each test has expected signal characteristics and pass/fail notes.

tasks.title: Collect first baseline session
acceptance_criteria: At least 15 minutes of continuous data is collected.
Session can be plotted and inspected in the labeling workbench.
Sequence gaps are documented.
Accel and gyro magnitudes are plausible.
Known activity periods are labeled through the workbench.

tasks.title: Write hardware wiring documentation
acceptance_criteria: Wiring table maps MPU-6050 VCC, GND, SCL, SDA, INT, and AD0 to ESP8266 pins.
Notes mention shared 3.3 V supply and shared ground.

tasks.title: Write API contract documentation
acceptance_criteria: Docs include endpoint list, request examples, response examples, validation rules, duplicate sequence behavior, and workbench API endpoints.
A firmware developer can implement against the API without reading server code.

tasks.title: Write payload schema documentation
acceptance_criteria: Docs define all fields, units, sample timing model, and sequence number purpose.
Data semantics are clear.

definition_of_done: Device uploads batched IMU samples to local FastAPI server.
Server stores raw samples in SQLite.
Duplicate batch sequences are handled explicitly.
Manual event labels can be created with session_id and device-relative timing.
Samples can be exported as CSV.
Session and event windows can be plotted.
Labels can be created, edited, and deleted from an interactive timeline workbench.
Sliding-window datasets can be generated from human-labeled time ranges.
At least one 15-minute baseline session is collected and reviewed.
Documentation exists for wiring, API, payload schema, labeling workflow, model design, and test procedure.
No seizure detection or medical alerting logic is implemented in V0.
